channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Pelicans players, coach grieve those killed in New Orleans attack as they play in Miami
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Trey Murphy III knew his mother was in downtown New Orleans to ring in the new year. His chef was there as well. And when Murphy heard about the tragedy that happened on Bourbon Street, he was scared for their safety. The word eventually came: They were fine.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Penn State takes simple approach during ride into CFP semifinals
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Football coaches, perhaps more than in other sports, are huge proponents of structure, routine, consistent messaging. Since the early days of his time in Happy Valley, Penn State's James Franklin has adhered to the philosophy of 1-0. Yes, it's a cliche.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sugar Bowl CFP quarterfinal between Georgia and Notre Dame postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame was postponed a day because of an attack about a mile away from the Superdome early Wednesday, when authorities say a truck driver deliberately plowed into a New Year’s crowd and killed 15 p
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Targeting no-call at Peach Bowl raises more questions about disputed rule
item.description: The Peach Bowl referee declined to call a targeting penalty late in the fourth quarter for a helmet-to-helmet hit by Texas safety Michael Taaffe that could’ve given Arizona State a chance to kick a winning field goal in the College Football Playoff quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: Paul Newberry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Skattebo's do-it-all show is not enough to overcome Arizona State's 4th-down woes against Texas
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Cam Skattebo's do-it-all offensive show wasn't enough to save Arizona State from a stretch of fourth-down failures against Texas. Led by Skattebo, the senior running back, Arizona State compiled big advantages in rushing yards, total yards and time of possession.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sugar Bowl announces new start time after postponement amid New Orleans attack
item.description: The Allstate Sugar Bowl announced that the game will start at 4 p.m. ET on Thursday after Wednesday's deadly attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Georgia student 'critically injured' in New Orleans attack ahead of Sugar Bowl game
item.description: University of Georgia president Jere W. Morehead confirmed that one student was "critically injured" during an apparent terror attack in New Orleans on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas avoids huge upset, beats Arizona State 39-31 in double overtime at Peach Bowl
item.description: Texas avoided the first big upset of the College Football Playoff, hitting a fourth-and-long touchdown pass to keep the game going and beating Arizona State 39-31 in double overtime when an interception by Andrew Mukuba finally ended a Peach Bowl quarterfinal classic.
item.dc:creator: Paul Newberry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White Sox trade for left-handed reliever from Phillies
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago White Sox traded for a left-handed reliever from the Philadelphia Phillies in exchange for a minor league prospect. In exchange for minor-league right-hander Aaron Combs, the White Sox acquired 31-year-old left-handed pitcher Tyler Gilbert from the Phillies.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday. Coach Sean McVay announced the decision Wednesday to rest Stafford, the 16-year veteran starter.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah HC loses fifth straight game, 4-1 to Edmonton
item.description: EDMONTON (ABC4 Sports) - Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl each extended their point streaks to 12 games for the Edmonton Oilers, who won 4-1 against the Utah Hockey Club at Rogers Place on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: No. 5 Penn State bottles up Jeanty, beats No. 8 Boise State 31-14 in Fiesta Bowl
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Penn State defenders clogged every hole, stretched out every outside run, closed with ferocity. Everywhere that Ashton Jeanty turned, he ran into Nittany Lions. The Heisman Trophy runner-up still went over 100 yards, but he sure had to work for it.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah's road winning streak ends in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) - Jaden Schwartz had a goal and two assists for the Seattle Kraken, who pulled away in the third period for a 5-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Climate Pledge Arena on Monday. The loss ends Utah's seven-game road winning streak. “[Points] never come easy,” Schwartz said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan defense pressures Milroe all day, Wolverines beat No. 11 Alabama 19-13 in ReliaQuest Bowl
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — A year removed from winning the national championship, Michigan finished its first season under Sherrone Moore with a victory setting the tone for a bright future. Moore replaced Jim Harbaugh after the former Wolverines coach left for the NFL's Los Angeles Chargers last winter.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington Commanders complete a turnaround from 4-13 to making the playoffs behind Jayden Daniels
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — Moments after the Washington Commanders finished the 2023 season at 4-13 and locked up the second pick in the draft, Daron Payne was already feeling optimistic about the organization's long-term future.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Chicago Blackhawks brought the St. Louis Blues to life
item.description: As the St. Louis Blues and Chicago Blackhawks gear up for the 2025 NHL Winter Classic, it's worth revisiting a fascinating piece of hockey history: The Blues franchise, as St. Louis knows it today, might not exist without a helping hand from their cross-state rival.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: SDFNL All-Star Showcase
item.description:
Boys All-Star Game : Blue Stars 21- Red Stars 17
Girls Flag Football All-Star Game: Blue Stars 6 - Red Stars 0
San Diego 13U Youth Super Bowl: San Diego Bolts 34 - Oceanside Warriors 22
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Jayden Daniels dazzles again as Commanders clinch a playoff spot by beating Falcons 30-24 in OT
item.description: Jayden Daniels connected with Zach Ertz in overtime for his third touchdown pass of the game to clinch a playoff spot by beating the Atlanta Falcons 30-24 in overtime Sunday night in a back-and-forth prime-time showdown between highly drafted rookie quarterbacks.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2024 Holiday Bowl: Homegrown Cougars leave it all on the field in front of hometown crowd
item.description: While a depleted Washington State may not have tasted any eggnog this year in the Holiday Bowl after falling short to Syracuse 52-35, a few San Diego locals still making a memory of a lifetime playing on the big stage in front of their hometown crowd.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Column: The path of Matthew Stafford looms for Caleb Williams
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — A skilled quarterback burdened by the dysfunction of his organization, doomed to toil in mediocrity for over a decade despite a high level of performance year in and year out.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Cathedral Catholic goalie shows why she's a keeper; honors her coach through art
item.description: Cathedral Catholic's 4-year starting goalie Maria'O Brien expresses her love to her head soccer coach through art. Suprising and touching longtime Cathedral Coach Dawn Lee.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Bears lose rain-soaked rock fight to the Seahawks, drop franchise record-tying 10th straight game
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears final home game of the season against the Seattle Seahawks was offense optional, as rain poured down on Soldier Field in what amounted to a cold, wet rock fight. "I thought special teams played very well.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
